notes: ARM-based processor; 233 MHz delivering highest Java CaffeineMark scores. Sold to Intel in 1997 patent settlement; Intel shipped SA-1100 in 1998; succeeded by XScale (2000).
Comprehensive NC software: NC Server, NC Access ROM OS, NC Card smartcard, NC Applications. Discontinued in 1999 when NCI was shut down.
Standard hardware specification for Network Computers. Attracted IBM, Sun. Concept succeeded by thin-client and cloud standards.
Intel/Microsoft response to NC; restricted expansion slots, remote management. Did not achieve significant market adoption; superseded by modern managed PCs.
Central to NC concept. Java performance of StrongARM was a key NC selling point. Java remains active under Oracle ownership.
ISO-standard smart card for NC user authentication. Concept was ahead of its time; similar approaches now in use (PIV, FIDO2).
BSD Unix-based NC server OS for small businesses. Discontinued with NCI shutdown.
64-bit Unix OS for Alpha servers. Continued as Tru64 UNIX under Compaq/HP until support ended ~2012.
